rationale: Both candidates converge on the same core structural point the ground truth hints at (dsocek's suggestion to derive component names from an existing mapping rather than hardcoding a list), though neither cites the exact `_packed_modules_mapping`/PEFT-naming-conflict angle dsocek raised, capping recall for both. X is better calibrated: it correctly lands on an approve-level COMMENT verdict (matching the actual outcome of two human LGTMs) and adds a second genuinely useful, well-grounded finding (text-encoder LoRA remaining a silent no-op) that mirrors the same failure mode the PR fixes. Y's output is marred by verbatim triplication of its own review text and issues a REQUEST CHANGES verdict for findings it itself labels minor/nit, an internal miscalibration that hurts precision despite solid grounding and evidence citations in the findings themselves.
